generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) match the expected expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` helpers to define reusable expressions within AGREE contracts.
Value-in-range patterns use boolean expressions: `value_in >= lower_limit and value_in <= upper_limit`.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce unqualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model.
Avoid adding architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on fixing the contract itself.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope.
